summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall exfiltrates developer identity, SCM, and cloud account data to attacker endpoint
details: anthropic-toolkit@0.5.1 impersonates Anthropic branding ('TypeScript utilities for the Anthropic SDK') but ships no library code — the declared main entry dist/index.js is absent from the tarball. The only executable content is scripts/postinstall.js, registered as the package's postinstall lifecycle hook, which fires automatically on `npm install`. The script harvests installer-side identity and configuration: os.hostname(), os.userInfo() username, Windows domain\user, git user.email from ~/.gitconfig and ~/.config/git/config, GitHub CLI login and email from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, SSH public-key comment emails from ~/.ssh/*.pub, git reflog committer emails (walking up to 10 parent directories for.git/config and.git/logs/HEAD), GCP project and account from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, DNS search domain from /etc/resolv.conf, cwd, CI provider, and parent project package.json metadata. The collected JSON payload is POSTed via https.request to the hardcoded endpoint https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. An in-code comment frames the behavior as 'anonymous diagnostics', contradicted by the identity-grade payload contents. Execution is gated only by an opt-out environment variable. The package's name, missing library code, non-existent GitHub repo, and harvester-only content together identify it as a deliberate brand-impersonation lure built to collect developer and organizational identity at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
